info.title: Production Scheduling Web Services
info.description:
Public API exposed by the DELMIA Production Scheduling cloud Service.
Version for 25X FD03 release.
info.summary: REST services for managing the production plans in DELMIA Production operations Scheduling.

PSL.description: URL of the DELMIA Production Scheduling service.

parameters.description: Required header for CSRF protection. mandatory value is "Batch". Another condition is that the Origin header should not be set.

schema.description: Production plan with mandatory attributes for creation.

id.description:
Unique identifier of the production plan.
Word characters only (a word character is a character from a-z, A-Z, 0-9, including the _ (underscore) character).
Max length: 40.

allOf.description: Production plan is the root object, direct or idirect owner of all the others.

name.description: Human readable identifier. For UX purpose, should better be unique.

label.description: For UX purpose, description of the production plan.
examples: details on localization, produced goods, etc...

timezone.description: Timezone applicable to the plant. See https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_tz_database_time_zones

horizonStart.description: Planning start date. No operation (unless already started) can start before this date.
horizonStart.default: current datetime in the selected timezone

schedulingType.description:
The scheduling type is an optional attribute of the resources. This attribute is available also for the production plan, where it serves as a default for all the resources that do not have a scheduling type.</BR>
Use detailed scheduling for bottleneck resources or when optimizing the setup duration is important.</BR>
Resources that need more flexibility in the organization of their work can use capacity scheduling type.
Value | Description
- | -
DETAILED | The work order operations are precisely sequenced one after the other on the resource.
CAPACITY | The work load of the work order operations is assigned to capacity buckets. The size of the buckets is defined in the planningPeriodDefinition in the "Calendars" section.

schedulingSpace.description:
Value in seconds.
The scheduling space is an optional attribute of the resources and work order operations. This attribute is available also for the production plan, where it serves as a default for all the resources that do not have a scheduling space.
The scheduling space is an additional duration applied to all operations assigned to capacity-scheduled resources. It adds lead time to the operation but gives flexibility for the resource to organize its work.'

parameters.name: X-Requested-With
parameters.description: Required header for CSRF protection. mandatory value is "Batch". Another condition is that the Origin header should not be set.

plannedDowntimes.description: Represent days off for the whole plant, overriding the "normal" calendar. Typical use is for public holidays. In case of update/delete, the whole array is sent again to replace the previous elements.

planningPeriodDefinition.description:
Splits the timeline into buckets. The planning period is applicable to all resource set to CAPACITY scheduling type.</BR>
The planning horizon is made of buckets of each time unit, from the smaller (hour) to the bigger (week) in the given number.</BR>
The attributes startMinuteOfHour, startHourOfDay and startDayOfWeek are used to configure an anchor for the alignment of the buckets.</BR>
With this definition, the planning horizon is limited. Beyond the last weekly bucket, the capacity-scheduled resources are considered as infinite capacity.

hours.description: number of buckets of 1 hour long, determining the duration of the hourly buckets period.

shifts.description: number of buckets of 8 hours long, determining the duration of the "shift" buckets period. A shift is basically a third of a day.

days.description: number of buckets of 1 day long, determining the duration "daily" buckets period.

weeks.description: number of buckets of 7 days long, determining the duration "weekly" buckets period.

startMinuteOfHour.description: Starting minute within a hour. E.G. set this attribute to 30, all buckets will start at half past X. This value is also applicable to the capacity cycles.

startHourOfDay.description: Starting hour within a day. E.G. set this attribute to 2 if the first shift of the day starts at 2AM (minutes are defined in startMinuteOfHour). This value is also applicable to the capacity cycles.

startDayOfWeek.description: Starting day of the week. 1 is MONDAY and 7 is SUNDAY. The weekly buckets are aligned with this setting.

shiftPatterns.description:
Represent the absolute version of a resource default calendar, based on a standard week.
Can be easily shared between multiple resources.
Must be replicated for each week on the optimization horizon and aggregated with exceptions to obtain the actual resource calendar.

items.description: Describes working hours inside a week. Only opened periods are listed. The closed periods are the gaps between the listed opened periods.

id.description: Unique Identifier of the calendar.

startDay.description: Start day of the week. Monday is 1 and Sunday is 7.

startTime.description: Start time in the starting day. Number of seconds from midnight. Interpreted as local time, according to the production plan''s timezone.

endDay.description: End day of the week. Monday is 1 and Sunday is 7.

endTime.description: End time in the ending day.  Number of seconds from midnight. Interpreted as local time, according to the production plan''s timezone.

items.description: Definition of capacity over time, used for resource pools. The capacity defined here will then apply to the buckets defined in the planning period definition. By the way, the attributes startMinuteOfHour and startHourOfDay set on the planning period definition are also applicable to capacity cycles, but that's not the case for startDayOfWeek.

id.description: Unique Identifier of the calendar.

periodType.description:
Defines the duration of each period in the cycle.
Value | Description
- | -
HOUR | Each period represent a 1 hour timespan. For fine grained capacity definition.
SHIFT | Each period represent a 8 hours timespan. Typical duration of a shift in a 3 shifts organization.
DAY | Each period represent a 24 hours timespan.
WEEK | Each period represent a 7 days timespan. For rough capacity definition.

cycleStartDay.description: Starting day of the cycle. 1 is MONDAY and 7 is SUNDAY.

periods.description: Ordered array. The order of the items is relevant to build the calendar representation.

items.description: Period in a capacityCycle. The capacity of the period is computed following the formula duration * copies * throughput/100

duration.description: Capacity available in this period per copy. Expressed in seconds. Since the capacity is supposed to model the workforce of a pool of resources, this duration may represent more time than the period duration.

copies.description: Number of copies of the resource in the pool.

throughput.description: Represents the ratio between the average workload that can be executed per available time of the workforce. Expressed as a percentage.

It should contains all the relevant occurrences for the expected planning horizon each time it is posted.

parameters.description: Required header for CSRF protection. mandatory value is "Batch". Another condition is that the Origin header should not be set.

allOf.description: A changeover activity is used to represent the steps required to prepare an asset to execute an operation. It usually depends on what was the previous operation executed on the same asset. There might be zero to many changeover activities applicable to the same asset. Some of these activities may not be relevant in some situations (combination of parameter values) and if so, the changeover duration may be set to 0.

id.description: Unique identifier of the activity.

aggregation.description:
Value | Description
 - | -
 sum | The changeover durations are cumulative. Applicable when each parameter is related to an activity and those activities are executed in sequence, one after the other.
 max | Only the maximum changeover duration among all parameters is applied. Applicable when changeover activities are executed all at the same time, in parallel.
 formula | The aggregation of all individual parameter changeovers is determined by a custom formula.

aggregationFormula.description: Apply MAX and SUM operators. The operands are the parameter ids.

externalData.description: Additional content that should not be relevant for the optimizer and not sent to the propagation engine, except for persistency.

allOf.description:
Considering 2 conscutive operations assigned to the same asset, the rule compares the value of the parameter of the previous operation (from) and the current operation (to).
The computed changeover is the first one that matches the "from" and "to" values, considering in order:
- the list of exceptions (first one that matches)
- defaultChangeOverIfEqual if "from" and "to" are equal (tolerance applies for numeric values)
- defaultChangeOver

name.description: Name of the parameter. Used as a lookup key from changeoverRules.

tolerance.description: If the difference between "from" and "to" are inferior or equal to the tolerance, both values are considered equal.

exceptions.description: An exception is applicable if the "from" value is included between fromRangeStart and fromRangeEnd, and the "to" value is included between toRangeStart and toRangeEnd.

fromRangeStartIncluded.description: If true, the boundary is included in the range, otherwise it's excluded.

genericId.description:
Identifier of the generic resource. Generic resources may be used in the operations' capable resources.
In this case, all the assets/pools having the same genericId are considered as capable to execute the operation.

workcenterId.description: Globally unique identifier. In order to satisfy the "globally unique" condition, the id may have to be constructed as the concatenation of multiple attributes from the source system, such as its type and its primary key attributes.

schedulingType.description:
The scheduling type is an optional attribute of the resources. This attribute is available also for the production plan, where it serves as a default for all the resources that do not have a scheduling type.</BR>
Use detailed scheduling for bottleneck resources or when optimizing the setup duration is important.</BR>
Resources that need more flexibility in the organization of their work can use capacity scheduling type.
Value | Description
- | -
DETAILED | The work order operations are precisely sequenced one after the other on the resource.
CAPACITY | The work load of the work order operations is assigned to capacity buckets. The size of the buckets is defined in the planningPeriodDefinition in the "Calendars" section.

schedulingSpace.description:
Value in seconds.
The scheduling space is an optional attribute of the resources and work order operations. This attribute is available also for the production plan, where it serves as a default for all the resources that do not have a scheduling space.
The scheduling space is an additional duration applied to all operations assigned to capacity-scheduled resources. It adds lead time to the operation but gives flexibility for the resource to organize its work.'

changeoverActivityIds.description: List of changeover activities applicable to this asset. All these activities can be executed in parallel, meaning that the changeover duration of an operation executed on this asset will correspond to the longest of these activities.

items.description: Unique identifier of the activity.

icon.description: Resource path to the icon to display.

timeCalendars.description: List of week pattern assignments from a start to an end date-time. Overlap between assignments is resolved by priority. When this attribute is missing or the array is empty, the asset is considered OPEN full time.

items.description: Time calendar applied to a resource between the start and end dates. Only 1 assignment per resource should have no end date. This one is the "default" pattern to apply whenever there is no higher-priority assignment.

allOf.description: Time calendar reference.
Calendar to apply between the start and end dates. If the end date is not specified, that means that it's the default calendar to apply whenever there is no other assignment.

text.description: User defined information. E.G. the cause of the exception.
examples: Machine broken. Waiting for spare parts delivery.

priority.description: Priority used in case of overlap of 2 or more assignments to the same resource. The higher (alphabetic sort) overrides the lower.

examples: Machine broken. Waiting for spare parts delivery.

priority.description: Priority used in case of overlap of 2 or more assignments to the same resource. The higher (alphabetic sort) overrides the lower.

capacityExceptions.description: List of capacity exceptions between dates. In case of an overlap with a cycle, exceptions always override underlying cycles. In case of an overlap with other exceptions, the one having the highest priority overrides the other(s).

items.description: Period for the capacity on a resource.

capacityDefinition.description: Period in a capacityCycle. The capacity of the period is computed following the formula duration * copies * throughput/100

duration.description: Capacity available in this period per copy. Expressed in seconds. Since the capacity is supposed to model the workforce of a pool of resources, this duration may represent more time than the period duration.

copies.description: Number of copies of the resource in the pool.

throughput.description: Represents the ratio between the average workload that can be executed per available time of the workforce. Expressed as a percentage.

post.summary: Creates or updates the work orders.
post.description: If a work order is posted with this method but the same id already exist, the content of the work order will be entirely replaced by the new version (delete followed by create). Thus, flows starting or ending on this work order or any of its operations should be sent along to be restored. Please use the patch method if the intent is to modify an existing order''s attributes or one of its operations individually.

proportionalToQty.description:
If true, the WO operation execution duration will be 
(durationRuc * quantity / ruc + preparationTime).
durationRuc and ruc depend on found on the capable resource. Quantity is set on the work order.
If false, the WO operation execution duration will be fixed/will not depend on its quantity. It will be just 
(durationRuc + preparationTime).

durationRuc.description: Seconds. Duration required to produces the quantity specified by ruc.

preparationTime.description: Seconds. Constant part of the execution time, does not depend on the quantity.

tomBefore.description: Seconds. Time out of machine before the execution. Typically: quality or security check.

tomAfter.description: Seconds. Time out of machine after the execution. Typically: wait for paint to dry.

interruptible.description: If true, the operation can be stopped by an calendar break on its asset.

schedulingSpace.description:
Value in seconds.
The scheduling space is an optional attribute of the resources and work order operations. This attribute is available also for the production plan, where it serves as a default for all the resources that do not have a scheduling space.
The scheduling space is an additional duration applied to all operations assigned to capacity-scheduled resources. It adds lead time to the operation but gives flexibility for the resource to organize its work.'

toolChangeoverTime.description: Seconds. Setup duration applied if the previous operation on the same asset does not require the same set of secondary resources flagged as "impactToolChangeover".

secondaryResNeed.description: Secondary resources needed to execute the operation on the parent primary resource.

secondaryResourceId.description: Unique Identifier of the resource or the generic Resource

impactToolChangeover.description: Indicates whether or not this resource is a tool for the tool changeover calculation.

timeCoefficient.description: This duration is the capacity load on the resource pool when producing the quantity specified by operation ruc. Expressed in seconds.

timeConstant.description: Constant added to calculate the load in time. Expressed in seconds.

alerts.description: List of issues met by the scheduling logic. That may include missing reference data (E.G. a resource uses an unknown shift pattern id), disabled constraints (E.G. flows creating a loop are forbidden. One must be disabled to break the circular reference.) and operations impossible to schedule (E.G. There is no resource in the plan capable to satisfy a requirement).

post.summary: Resets the schedule to a specific state.
post.description: Usually used to cancel changes and reset to a previous shedule. Unschedule all operations and reschedule only those in the request body, on the given assigned resource and following the sequence given by the start dates. The operations that are already in progress or completed are not affected by this method and will just be ignored if present in the input body. Any operation in the production plan that is not in the input body will be left unscheduled.

prop_horizonStart.description: Planning start date. No operation (unless already started) can start before this date.
prop_horizonStart.default: current datetime in the selected timezone

prop_schedulingType.description:
The scheduling type is an optional attribute of the resources. This attribute is available also for the production plan, where it serves as a default for all the resources that do not have a scheduling type.</BR>
Use detailed scheduling for bottleneck resources or when optimizing the setup duration is important.</BR>
Resources that need more flexibility in the organization of their work can use capacity scheduling type.
Value | Description
- | -
DETAILED | The work order operations are precisely sequenced one after the other on the resource.
CAPACITY | The work load of the work order operations is assigned to capacity buckets. The size of the buckets is defined in the planningPeriodDefinition in the "Calendars" section.

prop_schedulingSpace.description:
Value in seconds.
The scheduling space is an optional attribute of the resources and work order operations. This attribute is available also for the production plan, where it serves as a default for all the resources that do not have a scheduling space.
The scheduling space is an additional duration applied to all operations assigned to capacity-scheduled resources. It adds lead time to the operation but gives flexibility for the resource to organize its work.'

plannedDowntimes.description: Represent days off for the whole plant, overriding the "normal" calendar. Typical use is for public holidays. In case of update/delete, the whole array is sent again to replace the previous elements.

planningPeriodDefinition.description:
Splits the timeline into buckets. The planning period is applicable to all resource set to CAPACITY scheduling type.</BR>
The planning horizon is made of buckets of each time unit, from the smaller (hour) to the bigger (week) in the given number.</BR>
The attributes startMinuteOfHour, startHourOfDay and startDayOfWeek are used to configure an anchor for the alignment of the buckets.</BR>
With this definition, the planning horizon is limited. Beyond the last weekly bucket, the capacity-scheduled resources are considered as infinite capacity.

hours.description: number of buckets of 1 hour long, determining the duration of the hourly buckets period.

shifts.description: number of buckets of 8 hours long, determining the duration of the "shift" buckets period. A shift is basically a third of a day.

days.description: number of buckets of 1 day long, determining the duration "daily" buckets period.

weeks.description: number of buckets of 7 days long, determining the duration "weekly" buckets period.

startMinuteOfHour.description: Starting minute within a hour. E.G. set this attribute to 30, all buckets will start at half past X. This value is also applicable to the capacity cycles.

startHourOfDay.description: Starting hour within a day. E.G. set this attribute to 2 if the first shift of the day starts at 2AM (minutes are defined in startMinuteOfHour). This value is also applicable to the capacity cycles.

startDayOfWeek.description: Starting day of the week. 1 is MONDAY and 7 is SUNDAY. The weekly buckets are aligned with this setting.

startDay.description: Start day of the week. Monday is 1 and Sunday is 7.

startTime.description: Start time in the starting day. Number of seconds from midnight. Interpreted as local time, according to the production plan''s timezone.

endDay.description: End day of the week. Monday is 1 and Sunday is 7.

endTime.description: End time in the ending day.  Number of seconds from midnight. Interpreted as local time, according to the production plan''s timezone.

ShiftPattern.description: Describes working hours inside a week. Only opened periods are listed. The closed periods are the gaps between the listed opened periods.

CapacityPeriod.description: Period in a capacityCycle. The capacity of the period is computed following the formula duration * copies * throughput/100

duration.description: Capacity available in this period per copy. Expressed in seconds. Since the capacity is supposed to model the workforce of a pool of resources, this duration may represent more time than the period duration.

copies.description: Number of copies of the resource in the pool.

throughput.description: Represents the ratio between the average workload that can be executed per available time of the workforce. Expressed as a percentage.

CapacityCycle.description: Definition of capacity over time, used for resource pools. The capacity defined here will then apply to the buckets defined in the planning period definition. By the way, the attributes startMinuteOfHour and startHourOfDay set on the planning period definition are also applicable to capacity cycles, but that's not the case for startDayOfWeek.

id.description: Unique Identifier of the calendar.

periodType.description:
Defines the duration of each period in the cycle.
Value | Description
- | -
HOUR | Each period represent a 1 hour timespan. For fine grained capacity definition.
SHIFT | Each period represent a 8 hours timespan. Typical duration of a shift in a 3 shifts organization.
DAY | Each period represent a 24 hours timespan.
WEEK | Each period represent a 7 days timespan. For rough capacity definition.

cycleStartDay.description: Starting day of the cycle. 1 is MONDAY and 7 is SUNDAY.

periods.description: Ordered array. The order of the items is relevant to build the calendar representation.

Identifier of the parameter within this collection.
Word characters only (a word character is a character from a-z, A-Z, 0-9, including the _ (underscore) character).

ParameterName.description: Name of the parameter. Used as a lookup key from changeoverRules.

ParameterRule.description:
Considering 2 conscutive operations assigned to the same asset, the rule compares the value of the parameter of the previous operation (from) and the current operation (to).
The computed changeover is the first one that matches the "from" and "to" values, considering in order:
- the list of exceptions (first one that matches)
- defaultChangeOverIfEqual if "from" and "to" are equal (tolerance applies for numeric values)
- defaultChangeOver

ChangeoverActivity.description: A changeover activity is used to represent the steps required to prepare an asset to execute an operation. It usually depends on what was the previous operation executed on the same asset. There might be zero to many changeover activities applicable to the same asset. Some of these activities may not be relevant in some situations (combination of parameter values) and if so, the changeover duration may be set to 0.

id.description: Unique identifier of the activity.

aggregation.description:
Value | Description
 - | -
 sum | The changeover durations are cumulative. Applicable when each parameter is related to an activity and those activities are executed in sequence, one after the other.
 max | Only the maximum changeover duration among all parameters is applied. Applicable when changeover activities are executed all at the same time, in parallel.
 formula | The aggregation of all individual parameter changeovers is determined by a custom formula.

aggregationFormula.description: Apply MAX and SUM operators. The operands are the parameter ids.

externalData.description: Additional content that should not be relevant for the optimizer and not sent to the propagation engine, except for persistency.

schedulingType.description:
The scheduling type is an optional attribute of the resources. This attribute is available also for the production plan, where it serves as a default for all the resources that do not have a scheduling type.</BR>
Use detailed scheduling for bottleneck resources or when optimizing the setup duration is important.</BR>
Resources that need more flexibility in the organization of their work can use capacity scheduling type.
Value | Description
- | -
DETAILED | The work order operations are precisely sequenced one after the other on the resource.
CAPACITY | The work load of the work order operations is assigned to capacity buckets. The size of the buckets is defined in the planningPeriodDefinition in the "Calendars" section.

schedulingSpace.description:
Value in seconds.
The scheduling space is an optional attribute of the resources and work order operations. This attribute is available also for the production plan, where it serves as a default for all the resources that do not have a scheduling space.
The scheduling space is an additional duration applied to all operations assigned to capacity-scheduled resources. It adds lead time to the operation but gives flexibility for the resource to organize its work.'

allOf.description: Calendar to apply between the start and end dates. If the end date is not specified, that means that it's the default calendar to apply whenever there is no other assignment.

text.description: User defined information. E.G. the cause of the exception.
examples: Machine broken. Waiting for spare parts delivery.

priority.description: Priority used in case of overlap of 2 or more assignments to the same resource. The higher (alphabetic sort) overrides the lower.

capacityExceptions.description: List of capacity exceptions between dates. In case of an overlap with a cycle, exceptions always override underlying cycles. In case of an overlap with other exceptions, the one having the highest priority overrides the other(s).

items.description: Period for the capacity on a resource.

capacityDefinition.description: Period in a capacityCycle. The capacity of the period is computed following the formula duration * copies * throughput/100

duration.description: Capacity available in this period per copy. Expressed in seconds. Since the capacity is supposed to model the workforce of a pool of resources, this duration may represent more time than the period duration.

copies.description: Number of copies of the resource in the pool.

throughput.description: Represents the ratio between the average workload that can be executed per available time of the workforce. Expressed as a percentage.

allOf.description: Calendar to apply between the start and end dates. If the end date is not specified, that means that it's the default calendar to apply whenever there is no other assignment.

text.description: User defined information. E.G. the cause of the exception.
examples: Machine broken. Waiting for spare parts delivery.

priority.description: Priority used in case of overlap of 2 or more assignments to the same resource. The higher (alphabetic sort) overrides the lower.

proportionalToQty.description:
If true, the WO operation execution duration will be 
(durationRuc * quantity / ruc + preparationTime).
durationRuc and ruc depend on found on the capable resource. Quantity is set on the work order.
If false, the WO operation execution duration will be fixed/will not depend on its quantity. It will be just 
(durationRuc + preparationTime).

durationRuc.description: Seconds. Duration required to produces the quantity specified by ruc.

preparationTime.description: Seconds. Constant part of the execution time, does not depend on the quantity.

tomBefore.description: Seconds. Time out of machine before the execution. Typically: quality or security check.

tomAfter.description: Seconds. Time out of machine after the execution. Typically: wait for paint to dry.

interruptible.description: If true, the operation can be stopped by an calendar break on its asset.

schedulingSpace.description:
Value in seconds.
The scheduling space is an optional attribute of the resources and work order operations. This attribute is available also for the production plan, where it serves as a default for all the resources that do not have a scheduling space.
The scheduling space is an additional duration applied to all operations assigned to capacity-scheduled resources. It adds lead time to the operation but gives flexibility for the resource to organize its work.'

proportionalToQty.description:
If true, the WO operation execution duration will be 
(durationRuc * quantity / ruc + preparationTime).
durationRuc and ruc depend on found on the capable resource. Quantity is set on the work order.
If false, the WO operation execution duration will be fixed/will not depend on its quantity. It will be just 
(durationRuc + preparationTime).

durationRuc.description: Seconds. Duration required to produces the quantity specified by ruc.

preparationTime.description: Seconds. Constant part of the execution time, does not depend on the quantity.

tomBefore.description: Seconds. Time out of machine before the execution. Typically: quality or security check.

tomAfter.description: Seconds. Time out of machine after the execution. Typically: wait for paint to dry.

interruptible.description: If true, the operation can be stopped by an calendar break on its asset.

schedulingSpace.description:
Value in seconds.
The scheduling space is an optional attribute of the resources and work order operations. This attribute is available also for the production plan, where it serves as a default for all the resources that do not have a scheduling space.
The scheduling space is an additional duration applied to all operations assigned to capacity-scheduled resources. It adds lead time to the operation but gives flexibility for the resource to organize its work.'

proportionalToQty.description:
If true, the WO operation execution duration will be 
(durationRuc * quantity / ruc + preparationTime).
durationRuc and ruc depend on found on the capable resource. Quantity is set on the work order.
If false, the WO operation execution duration will be fixed/will not depend on its quantity. It will be just 
(durationRuc + preparationTime).

durationRuc.description: Seconds. Duration required to produces the quantity specified by ruc.

preparationTime.description: Seconds. Constant part of the execution time, does not depend on the quantity.

tomBefore.description: Seconds. Time out of machine before the execution. Typically: quality or security check.

tomAfter.description: Seconds. Time out of machine after the execution. Typically: wait for paint to dry.

interruptible.description: If true, the operation can be stopped by an calendar break on its asset.

schedulingSpace.description:
Value in seconds.
The scheduling space is an optional attribute of the resources and work order operations. This attribute is available also for the production plan, where it serves as a default for all the resources that do not have a scheduling space.
The scheduling space is an additional duration applied to all operations assigned to capacity-scheduled resources. It adds lead time to the operation but gives flexibility for the resource to organize its work.'

That may include missing reference data (E.G. a resource uses an unknown shift pattern id), disabled constraints (E.G. flows creating a loop are forbidden. Value | Description
- | -
PEGGING | Allocation was proposed by the MRP system.
SCHEDULING | Allocation was confirmed or optimized by the scheduling engine.
EXECUTION | Allocation was firmed by the MOM system. Allocation at this status cannot be changed anymore in the scheduling system.

Assigned asset identifier.

startDate.description: Planned or execution start date, depending on the status value.

minStartDateAuto.description: minimum start date as computed by the optimizer. It can be used for example to satisfy a maximum delay with the next operation.

minStartDate.description: Earliest start date constraint applied to the operation. It can be used to delay an operation for any reason. The description attribute can be used to explain those reasons.

description.description: User input to explain the rationals behind this constraint. Limited to 255 characters.
examples: Raw material delivery delayed at customs.

alerts.description: List of issues met by the scheduling logic. That may include missing reference data (E.G. a resource uses an unknown shift pattern id), disabled constraints (E.G. flows creating a loop are forbidden. One must be disabled to break the circular reference.) and operations impossible to schedule (E.G. There is no resource in the plan capable to satisfy a requirement).

allOf.description: Error information provided if the transaction failed.

placeHolders.description: List of key/value pairs. keys can be used as placeholders in the error message. A contextualised message can then be obtained by replacing the {key} pattern with the corresponding value.

relatedObjects.description: List of type/id pairs. These IDs are used to lookup for the objects related to the alert.

type.description:
Value | Description
- | -
workOrder | Work order.
workOrderFlow | Time constraint between work orders.
operation | Work order operation.
flow | Time constraint between operations.
workCenter | Group of assets.
asset | Execution resource.
shiftPattern | Week calendar pattern.
changeoverActivity | Changeover activity.
item | Item.
supply | Supply.
warehouse | Warehouse, container for supply.
supplyAllocation | Supply allocation to a work order operation.
resourcePool | Pool of secondary resources.
capacityCycle | Capacity cycle for resource pools.
